entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM / Memory Consumption Tuning for Health...
entry.content:
Hi, Benoit,
To answer your question about:
"How long should I wait before the cleaning of the Health Monitoring would be
performed?"

So long as the Framework-DataReorg job is running, housekeeping should occur once a day -> for more information on this, see KBA 3416639 - How to configure the data retention (housekeeping) in SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring.

(I know you have created a case for the remaining problem of memory consumption not clearing.  Proceed there for the rest of your question.)
Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Output Management in TM - BRF+ or PPF?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We recently implemented TM but only the basic settings since the client is still not ready to move fully from LE to TM. However, they are looking to step by step leave more room for TM to handled things. On of these is the output management.

Currently, they are using the old NACE to determine the output types and to send the forms and we are discussing to move this to TM. However, I am struggling to understand if this can also be handled via BRF+ or if it needs to go through PPF. I have been reading a lot and it seems for TM the only way to process outputs is via PPF.

On the output control settings, I see the application object types available to be activated limited mostly to Sales Documents, Purchasing Documents, etc but nothing related to Shipments.  Did I understood correctly?

Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Possible to recover deleted documents?
entry.content:
Hi,  kjetiltynes,

Your question is very generic and SAP Cloud ALM is quite complex offering many use cases and different areas.  It would help if your post were more specific on exactly what documents you are looking to recover in order to engage someone familiar with that specific area.
With that being said, I know of no recycle bin nor archiving process that might allow you to recover deleted 'elements' in Cloud ALM.
In case you have a request for changes to this area, you can check currently logged requests in our Influence Campaign and up-vote any relevant to your request or you can feel free to add your ideas:

SAP Cloud ALM influence campaigns (ECRs) can be logged at:

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

How to Retrieve Warehouse Items with Serial Numbers and Supplier Information?
entry.content:
EN

Hi,

I want to do a query that will get all the items on a warehouse and will show the serial number if it exists and also the supplier, date when it was bought, price of the item and DocNum. The issue is that although I have the query that shows the items on the warehouse with the serial number I don't know how to relate this to the other tables to see the supplier information. 

ES

Hola,

Quiero realizar una consulta que obtenga todos los artículos de un almacén y muestre el número de serie si existe, además del proveedor, la fecha de compra, el precio del artículo y el número de documento. El problema es que, aunque tengo la consulta que muestra los artículos del almacén con el número de serie, no sé cómo relacionarlo con las otras tablas para ver la información del proveedor.

For now I have the following query:

 

SELECT 
    T2."ItemCode", 
    T2."ItemName", 
    T1."WhsCode", 
    T1."WhsName",
    IFNULL((SELECT (SUM(Y."InQty") - SUM(Y."OutQty"))
            FROM OINM Y 
            WHERE Y."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" AND Y."Warehouse" = T1."WhsCode"), 0) AS "Stock",
    IFNULL((SELECT (SUM(Y."TransValue")) 
            FROM OINM Y 
            WHERE Y."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" AND Y."Warehouse" = T1."WhsCode"), 0) AS "Costo Total",
    T3."ItmsGrpNam",
    T2."LastPurPrc" AS "UltimoPrecioCompra",
    T0."AvgPrice" AS "PrecioPromedio",
    O0."DistNumber" AS "SerialNumber"
FROM 
    OITM T2
    INNER JOIN OITW T0 ON T0."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode"
    INNER JOIN OWHS T1 ON T1."WhsCode" = T0."WhsCode"
    INNER JOIN OITB T3 ON T2."ItmsGrpCod" = T3."ItmsGrpCod"
    LEFT JOIN OSRN O0 
        ON O0."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" 
        AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM OSRQ O2 
                    WHERE O2."ItemCode" = O0."ItemCode" 
                    AND O2."SysNumber" = O0."SysNumber" 
                    AND O2."WhsCode" = '[%0]') 
WHERE 
    (SELECT (SUM(Y."InQty") - SUM(Y."OutQty")) 
     FROM OINM Y  
     WHERE Y."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" 
       AND Y."Warehouse" = T1."WhsCode") != 0
    AND T0."WhsCode" = '[%0]'
ORDER BY 
    T2."ItemCode", 
    T1."WhsCode";

Is it posible already to achieve what @scb-schozs mentioned on their comment, as in catching not only the error code but also the error message from the service?

There are cases where the services handle not only error code but error messages in a proper way. Specially when there are errors with the same come but the message can differ.

Since the service itslef handles properly the different execptions, it would make more sense to map this information direclty as Service Output, instead of doing another handling at Production Process level, with less capability of mapping detail.

Juan.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Analytics Average Calculation
entry.content:
Hi User Please check below points 


Verify the Calculated Measure Settings:

Go to the Calculated Measure settings.
Ensure that:
Operation is set to Average.
Aggregation Dimension is correctly set to Region.
Double-check that the measure used for the calculation is numeric and supports aggregation.
Review Chart Configuration:

Open the chart settings and ensure:
The chart type is appropriate for displaying averages (e.g., Bar, Column).
No conflicting aggregations are applied at the chart level.
The measure shown is the calculated measure you created.
Check the Model’s Aggregation Behavior:

Navigate to the Model in SAC and inspect the aggregation behavior of the base measure (e.g., Profit).
The default aggregation of the base measure (sum, average, etc.) should not conflict with the calculated measure.
Set Aggregation in the Chart:

In the chart designer:
Select the calculated measure.
Ensure that the aggregation is explicitly set to Average at the chart level.

Filter Settings:

Ensure that the filters applied to the chart or story do not inadvertently affect the calculation (e.g., Region filters might lead to unintended aggregation).
Use the Formula Editor:

If the predefined settings are not working as expected, use the Formula Editor to create a custom calculation for the average.

 

Example:
scss
Copy code
Average_Profit = SUM(Profit) / COUNT_DISTINCT([Region])
Replace [Region] with the appropriate dimension for your dataset.
Test in a Table Visualization:

Add a table to your story and include the calculated measure alongside Region.
Check if the calculated measure is showing the correct average for each region.
Refresh Data:

Refresh the model or data source to ensure the calculated measure is updated correctly.
Check for Story-Level Overrides:

Look for any story-level custom aggregations that might be overriding your calculated measure settings.

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Aleksei,

let me start to sort out the terms multi-tenant, multi-target and multi-backend:

Multi-tenancy refers to a solution architecture and deployment model tailored to SaaS solutions that you want to provide to many customers or consumers. It means you deploy your solution once with one database and one runtime, and then provide your solution to customers as subscriptions (tenants). In our reference example we are using the SAP Multi-Tenancy Extension (MTX) module, which is well-integrated with CAP and BTP services out-of-the-box.

A multi-target application is logically a single application comprised of multiple parts created with different technologies, which share the same lifecycle. In our reference example we are using the Multi-Target Application module (MTA) because is provides a standardized way to model the application structure and its dependencies. The MTA deployment service provides a very high degree of automation and ensures an automated, parallel and deterministic deployment/un-deployment, it automatically provisions required BTP services, it automatically creates routes, domains, and technical users, and it ensures a transactional consistency of deployments and recoverability, ... 

Multi-backend simply means that we designed our reference application as ERP-agnostic solution such that every subscription of the multi-tenant app can be connected to ERP system of the customer, regardless if it is an S/4HANA Cloud system, a B1 system, a ByD system or any other ERP.

Regarding the nature of the partner reference application: Yes it is definitely not designed as Hello World example, but rather explains how BTP services can be used and orchestrated to deliver enterprise-ready solutions that fit to the SAP standard in a scalable model. To get started with CAP, I would recommend the CAPire documentation with the "bookshop"-example. The Discovery Center provides you with a comprehensive catalog of use case specific missions. And last but not least I would recommend the learning journey Building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP as a good starting point as well.

entry.title: Unable to fetch KBETR field in price condition using formula routine
entry.content:
We have (zppt price per )tonne which is header condition type also we have ZB00 is base price item condition, we will enter manually zppt price at header and we have to calculate base price condition amount in KBETR ,we have created 918 condition value formula routine , we unable fetch zb00 condition rate in KBETR field, we are getting calculated XKWERT field i.e condition value, unable fetch KBETR, please suggest solution.

data : lt_pack TYPE TABLE OF zpack, wa_pack TYPE zpack.

data : lt_marm TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF marm, wa_marm type marm.

data : lt_marm1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF marm, wa_marm1 type marm.

data : lt_prcd TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF prcd_elements, wa_prcd type prcd_elements.

*DATA : ZB001 TYPE p DECIMALS 3.

DATA : ZB001 TYPE decfloat34.

DATA : ZB002 TYPE KWERT.

DATA : ZB003 TYPE KWERT.

data : lv_conversion TYPE f.

SELECT * FROM zpack INTO TABLE lt_pack WHERE matnr = komp-matnr and WERKS = komp-werks and meins = komp-meins.

SORT lt_pack DESCENDING by edate. SELECT * FROM marm INTO TABLE lt_marm where matnr = komp-matnr and meinh = 'TO'.

SELECT * from prcd_elements into table lt_prcd where knumv = komk-knumv and kschl = 'ZPPT'.

READ TABLE lt_marm into wa_marm INDEX 1. READ TABLE lt_pack into wa_pack INDEX 1. READ TABLE lt_prcd into wa_prcd with key kschl = 'ZPPT' kposn = komp-kposn.

ZB001 = ( wa_marm-umren / wa_marm-umrez ) .

IF KOMV-KSCHL = 'ZPPT'.

ZB002 = ZB001 * KOMV-KBETR .

ZB003 = ZB002 + wa_pack-rate.

xkwert = ZB003 * KOMP-MGAME / 1000 . ELSEIF

KOMV-KSCHL = 'ZB00'.

ZB002 = KOMV-kbetr.

xkwert = ZB002 * KOMP-MGAME / 1000 . ENDIF.

*ZB003 = ZB002 + wa_pack-rate.

xkwert = ZB003 KOMP-MGAME / 1000     above code is written in condition value formula routine

entry.title: Error HUGENERAL066 in LI20 During Inventory Write-Off
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are encountering an issue during the Physical Inventory (PI) process in transaction LI20 when attempting to write off inventory. The system generates error HUGENERAL066:

"Quantity 0 (not packed) is less than the requested quantity 4."

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Go into transaction LI20.
  2. Enter the inventory record and Warehouse, Variance in %: 10
  3. Click on “Write Off”.
  4. The following error message appears:
    • Quantity 0 (not packed) is less than the requested quantity 4.
    • Message No.: HUGENERAL066

Do you have any suggestions on how to resolve this error? Is there a configuration, process step, or master data issue that could be causing the discrepancy?

Thank you in advance for your support and insights.

entry.title: Re: LTI Template in SuccessFactors Compensation
entry.content:
Hi @satnaik18 

Please find more information below:

Screenshots and leading practice design of LTI template. 

Important Notes About Stock Planning Templates

Creating Stock Value Tables

Creating Stock Factor Tables

Creating Stock Participation Guideline Tables

 

 

Other things worth mentioning: we cannot move around column freely in the stock tab as we can in the Compensation tab. Any custom column we add will always show to the right of the stock recommendation columns. Which is often the reason why we end up using a dedicated Compensation template or a group of columns inside the Compensation tab to do stock planning (since we can do pretty much the same thing using lookup tables).

I have done 20+ implementations in the last 10 years and I configured the stock tab only once.

Hope this helps.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
As to the EOMM 2028 date, see the following question and Jason Noack's answer:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/no-sql-anywhere-ebf-in-5-months/qaq-p/13837725

According to that, SQL Anywhere is at least supported till 2028-12-31, and that date has been extended several times in the past (from 12/2023 to 12/2025 to 12/2027, IIRC...).

That being said, I strongly share the impression that less and less effort is put into enhancing the product. Nevertheless, for our needs and from a developer and part-time-DBA point of view, it does so many things much better than MS SQL Server, which we also use...

entry.title: Re: 3244147 - How to retrieve the classification data fro...
entry.content:
Hello @Tom1-2-3 

 

The KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3502310 outlines 2 ways to avoid getting the error :

 

Check the application component(business area) for a CDS view of the associated Data Source by steps below:

  1. Login to the Custom CDS view app, Select the scenario the Data Extraction.
  2. Click on add to add the primary data source using F4 help, kindly go to the option settings and enable the filter for component. 
  3. The field component will provide the application area to which the CDS view belongs.

There is a workaround: 

  1. Create a CDS view as scenario Cube or Dimension, with the same data sources.
  2. Create Data Extraction based on the created custom CDS view.

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Fiori & CAP: how can I prefill key field of draft-ena...
entry.content:
Your given solution is for java. I have found now a solution with CAP Node.js. 

I extend my CDS model with a function "getSystemTypesDefaults" and use the Common.DefautValuesFunction annotation approach.

CDS definition:

 

 

 

service AdminConfigurationService {

  .draft.enabled
  @Capabilities: {
    Insertable,
    Updatable,
    DeleteRestrictions: {Deletable: (isDeletable)}
  }

  entity SystemTypes       as projection on paltr.BaseSystemTypes;

  /**
     * Provides default values for systemtypes
  */
  function getSystemTypesDefaults() returns SystemTypes;
 ....
}

annotate AdminConfigurationService.SystemTypes with @(Common.DefaultValuesFunction: 'getSystemTypesDefaults');

 

 

 

  Within my TypeScript service file :

 

 

 

import cds, { User } from '@sap/cds';

class AdminConfigurationService extends cds.ApplicationService {
...
async init() {

    // extract entities
    const { SystemTypes } = this.entities;
// Register default value handler for key field ID

    this.on('getSystemTypesDefaults', async (req) => {
      let max_ID = await SELECT`max(ID) as max_ID`.from(SystemTypes);
      let max_ID2 = max_ID;

      if (!!SystemTypes.drafts) { max_ID2 = await SELECT`max(ID) as max_ID`.from(SystemTypes.drafts); }

      return { ID: Math.max(max_ID[0].max_ID, max_ID2[0].max_ID) + 1 };
    });

    await super.init();
  }
}

 

 

 

If I launch the app and press the standard button "create" on my listreport#s dynamic page, a popup is displayed and requires for key field values and other values as given in the Fiori UI annotations as follows:

 

 

 

 

 UI.FieldGroup #CreationDalogFieldGroup               : {
        $Type: 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [
                // {
                //     $Type                  : 'UI.DataField',
                //     Value                  : ID,
                //     ![@Common.FieldControl]: #ReadOnly
                // },
               {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: techName,
        }],
    },

 

 

 

Within the dialog, I can see the generated key field value from function "createSystemTypesDefaults": 

Now, how can I hide the key field input or at least disable the input in order to make the key field visible only ? Using the option #ReadOnly as given in the UI annotation above is not working.

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
The SQL Anywhere End of Mainstream Maintenance (EOMM) is 31 December 2028.

The Product Availability Matrix (PAM) is the definitive EOMM for SAP products. It is updated with any changes to EOMM dates.

entry.title: Re: How to master a successful on-premise H4S4 Migration: SAP ERP6 HCM to SAP HCM for S/4HANA (H4S4)
entry.content: SAP HCM for S/4HANA (H4S4) is not the successor solution to SAP ERP HCM on-premise. SAP SuccessFactors is the successor solution. H4S4 is just a bridge solution for companies who are not able to move to the cloud for regulatory or compliance reasons (e.g. they have special government requirements that require them to stay on-premise).

entry.title: Event Based Revenue Recognition Posting with Advance Intercompany Sales in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In this blog, I would like to show the posting schema of Event-based Revenue Recognition with Advance Intercompany Sales. The EBRR in this example is SFSCCM (defer costs and revenue at first and recognize the respective amounts after the final billing of the sales order).

For all the EBRR key in Sales Order, please refer to SAP Help here

Now, let's look into the system. We can check the Rev.Rec key assigned in the sales order with Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Sales Orders app

Monitor Value Flow will show end-to-end process of advance intercompany for this sales order.

During the Sales Order creation and Outbound Delivery, no Finance posting and no EBRR posting.

During the Good Issues for OD into Stock In Transit (movement type 687), Finance document is created. no EBRR posting since it is only to move from plant stock to stock in transit.

Now, the first EBRR posting happen at the GI to Customer step which we would have both Finance Posting (Inventory with COGS) and then EBRR posting to differ the COGS to WIP (because of the Rev. Rec. Key SFSCCM)

Accounting Document

Rev Rec Document for Ledger 0L

Then final step is when billing is created after POD is completed.

Accounting Document Post to Revenue Account

Rev Rec Document for Ledger 0L is now adjust to differ revenue (from Rev Rec Key logic)

Then at month end, we will perform Event-based Revenue Recognition - Sales Order. This can be done manually one by one like the example below or schedule a background job with Run Revenue Recognition – Sales Orders app

We can run Sales Order - Actual app to see end-to-end posting

Sort by Account then posting date, with this we can see that the WIP, Deferred cost and deferred revenue are cleared out. And we only see the real COGS and real Revenue for this sales order.

entry.title: SAPUI5 Custom Application: Digital Signature Draw Pad
entry.content:
Business Case

This blog showcases a custom SAP UI5 application that allows users to draw a signature on a digital pad, clear it, download it as an image (JPEG), or upload it to an SAP backend server for further processing. The application is built using the HTML5 control within the SAP UI5 framework.
It is designed to handle both desktop and touch devices such as mobile phones and tablets, ensuring broad usability across platforms.


Features

  • Draw Signature: Users can draw their signatures on a responsive digital canvas.

  • Clear Pad: The pad can be reset with a single click.

  • Download Signature: Users can save their signature locally as a JPEG file.

  • Upload to Server: The application provides functionality to save the signature to an SAP server for storage or further processing.

  • Touch Device Support: Fully functional on mobile devices and tablets with touch input support.

Application Structure

The application is implemented with a combination of SAP UI5 and HTML5 canvas features. Below is a breakdown of the technical components.

XML View

The XML view defines the structure and layout of the application, incorporating an HTML control for the canvas and buttons for actions:

 

<mvc:View controllerName="digitalsignature.controller.View1"
xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
xmlns="sap.m">
  <Page id="_IDGenPage" title="Digital Signature">
    <content>
      <VBox id="_IDGenVBox1" class="sapUiSmallMargin">
        <HBox id="_IDGenHBox">
          <Text id="_IDGenText" text="Draw your signature below:" />
        </HBox>
        <HBox id="_IDGenHBox1">
          <core:HTML id="signaturePad" content="{/signatureHTML}" afterRendering=".onAfterRenderingHTML" />
          <HBox alignItems="End" id="_IDGenHBox2" justifyContent="SpaceBetween" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop">
            <Button id="_IDGenButton1" text="Clear" press=".onClearPress" />
            <Button id="_IDGenButton2" text="Download as JPEG" press=".onDownloadPress" />
            <Button id="_IDGenButton3" text="Upload to Server" press=".onPostToServer" />
          </HBox>
        </HBox>
      </VBox>
    </content>
  </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

Controller Logic

The controller implements the core functionality of the application. It handles user interactions, such as drawing on the canvas, clearing it, and downloading the signature as an image.

Initialization and Canvas Rendering

 

onInit: function () {
    const signatureHTML = "<canvas id='signatureCanvas' width='500' height='300' style='border:1px solid #000;'></canvas>";
    const oModel = new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel({ signatureHTML: signatureHTML });
    this.getView().setModel(oModel);

    this._canvas = null;
    this._ctx = null;
    this._isDrawing = false;
},

onAfterRenderingHTML: function () {
    this._canvas = document.getElementById("signatureCanvas");

    if (this._canvas) {
        this._ctx = this._canvas.getContext("2d");
        this._setCanvasBackground();
        this._attachMouseEvents();
        this._attachTouchEvents();
    } else {
        console.error("Canvas element not found. Check your HTML content and IDs.");
    }
},

 

Drawing and Clearing Signature

 

_startDrawing: function (event) {
    this._isDrawing = true;
    this._ctx.beginPath();
    this._ctx.moveTo(event.offsetX, event.offsetY);
},

_draw: function (event) {
    if (!this._isDrawing) return;
    this._ctx.lineTo(event.offsetX, event.offsetY);
    this._ctx.strokeStyle = "#000";
    this._ctx.lineWidth = 2;
    this._ctx.stroke();
},

onClearPress: function () {
    if (this._canvas) {
        this._ctx.clearRect(0, 0, this._canvas.width, this._canvas.height);
        this._setCanvasBackground();
        MessageToast.show("Signature pad cleared.");
    }
},

 

Full Controller Code:

 

sap.ui.define([
   "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/m/MessageToast", 
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
],
    function (Controller, MessageToast, JSONModel) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("digitalsignature.controller.View1", {
            onInit: function () {
                const signatureHTML = "<canvas id='signatureCanvas' width='500' height='300' style='border:1px solid #000;'></canvas>";
                const oModel = new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel({ signatureHTML: signatureHTML });
                this.getView().setModel(oModel);

                this._canvas = null;
                this._ctx = null;
                this._isDrawing = false;
            },

            onAfterRenderingHTML: function () {
                this._canvas = document.getElementById("signatureCanvas");

                if (this._canvas) {
                    this._ctx = this._canvas.getContext("2d");
                    this._setCanvasBackground();
                    this._attachMouseEvents();
                    this._attachTouchEvents();
                } else {
                    console.error("Canvas element not found. Check your HTML content and IDs.");
                }
            },

            _setCanvasBackground: function () {
                this._ctx.fillStyle = "#FFFFFF"; // White background
                this._ctx.fillRect(0, 0, this._canvas.width, this._canvas.height);
            },

            _attachMouseEvents: function () {
                this._canvas.addEventListener("mousedown", this._startDrawing.bind(this));
                this._canvas.addEventListener("mousemove", this._draw.bind(this));
                this._canvas.addEventListener("mouseup", this._stopDrawing.bind(this));
                this._canvas.addEventListener("mouseleave", this._stopDrawing.bind(this));
            },

            _attachTouchEvents: function () {
                this._canvas.addEventListener("touchstart", this._startTouchDrawing.bind(this));
                this._canvas.addEventListener("touchmove", this._drawTouch.bind(this));
                this._canvas.addEventListener("touchend", this._stopDrawing.bind(this));
            },

            _startDrawing: function (event) {
                this._isDrawing = true;
                this._ctx.beginPath();
                this._ctx.moveTo(event.offsetX, event.offsetY);
            },

            _draw: function (event) {
                if (!this._isDrawing) return;
                this._ctx.lineTo(event.offsetX, event.offsetY);
                this._ctx.strokeStyle = "#000";
                this._ctx.lineWidth = 2;
                this._ctx.stroke();
            },

            _startTouchDrawing: function (event) {
                event.preventDefault(); // Prevent scrolling
                this._isDrawing = true;
                const touch = event.touches[0];
                const rect = this._canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
                const x = touch.clientX - rect.left;
                const y = touch.clientY - rect.top;
                this._ctx.beginPath();
                this._ctx.moveTo(x, y);
            },

            _drawTouch: function (event) {
                if (!this._isDrawing) return;
                event.preventDefault(); // Prevent scrolling
                const touch = event.touches[0];
                const rect = this._canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
                const x = touch.clientX - rect.left;
                const y = touch.clientY - rect.top;
                this._ctx.lineTo(x, y);
                this._ctx.strokeStyle = "#000";
                this._ctx.lineWidth = 2;
                this._ctx.stroke();
            },

            _stopDrawing: function () {
                this._isDrawing = false;
                this._ctx.closePath();
            },

            onClearPress: function () {
                if (this._canvas) {
                    this._ctx.clearRect(0, 0, this._canvas.width, this._canvas.height);
                    this._setCanvasBackground();
                    MessageToast.show("Signature pad cleared.");
                }
            },

            onDownloadPress: function () {
                if (this._canvas) {
                    const link = document.createElement("a");
                    link.href = this._canvas.toDataURL("image/jpeg");
                    link.download = "signature.jpeg";
                    link.click();
                    MessageToast.show("Signature downloaded as JPEG.");
                }
            },

            onPostToServer: function () {
                if (this._canvas) {
                    const imageData = this._canvas.toDataURL("image/jpeg");
                    // Add server upload logic here
                    MessageToast.show("Signature uploaded to server (mockup).");
                }
            }
        });
    });

 

Upload to SAP Server

While the implementation of server upload is included as a mockup, it can be achieved using the sap.ui.model.odata or fetch API. The signature can be sent as a base64 string to an SAP OData service.

Conclusion

This SAP UI5 custom application serves as a practical example of how HTML5 capabilities can be integrated within the SAP UI5 framework to meet real-world business requirements. The Digital Signature Draw Pad enhances user experience, supports digital transformation, and simplifies workflows. 

Feel free to implement this application in your SAP projects and customize it as per your needs.
Happy coding!😉

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integrationを利用したファイルの文字コード変換方法
entry.content:
Hi Shin,

Mine is reverse case like UTF-8 to Shift-JIS. i tried all the ways like through groovy also but it is work.

used groovy is 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets

def Message processData(Message message) {
// Get the body as a byte array (assuming it's in UTF-8)
def bodyBytes = message.getBody(byte[])

// Convert the UTF-8 byte array to a Shift_JIS string
def shiftJisString = new String(bodyBytes, StandardCharsets.UTF_8).getBytes("Shift_JIS")

// Set the converted Shift_JIS byte array as the new body
message.setBody(shiftJisString)

// Set the output charset to Shift_JIS
message.setProperty("CamelCharsetName", "Shift_JIS")

return message
}

But didnt get the CSV file in shift-JIS.

in notepad++ and Sakura english version, it is shwoing UTF-8 only.

i have attcahed the artifact screenshot alos.

groovyscript2 is for XML to CSV

groovyscript3 is for conversion which i gave above

Before groovyScript2 , XSLT mapping is there to arrange the XML based on the key.

 

Can you help me to achieve it in shift-JIS ?

Regards,

Bhavya K.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud infrastructure is moving to SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
@RWulfert, yes your understanding is correct, there have been a number of features released now that rely on the underlying infrastructure being based on HANA Cloud. For example Seamless Planning as a controlled release.

Many customers will have these available as we have been provisioning new customer tenants on latest HANA Cloud based infrastructure throughout 2024 and will continue to do so going forward.

For existing customer tenants, based on the HANA 2.0 infrastructure, we will begin migrating them in 2025. We understand this may be frustrating but we want to ensure that the migration causes minimum disruption to existing customer deployments while still delivering innovations that rely on the latest capabilities of HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Re: How to find/create suitable APIs for your extension and integration needs?
entry.content:
Very well written blog post, love it!

After reading & poking around the APIs within SAP EWM I've come to the conclusion that they're mostly of type 4 (the 4th point you described), where they do exist but with various limitations, not extensible at all (big 'NO' in the Accelerator Hub :P) and at times, even "discouraged" or rather only designed to be used within the Cloud. The same goes with most of the underlying CDS views and their contracts, as far as I could tell.

I was not so sure if that is a sign to once again "just do it yourself, if you really want it" (it being custom fields & logic with it) but reading through your blog post, I feel that is just the way it is. It is probably a more "special case", as it has always been with EWM (especially also with on-prem) but nevertheless, a bit sad. 🙂

Thanks again for this great write-up & Br,

Marco

entry.title: Business Application Studio-Fiori Tools - Fiori app generation broken for RAP-based OData V4 service
entry.content:
Hi,

Environment details:

Backend - SAP S4HANA 2022 FPS02

SAP Business Application Studio Version: 1.93.1
UI5 Version 1.108.30

I am encountering a strange issue in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) while generating a Fiori List Report for a RAP-based OData V4 service. Despite the service being explicitly defined and recognized as OData V4, BAS/Fiori Tools incorrectly treat it as an OData V2 service and generate faulty configurations.

This issue affects all my previously deployed V4 services, which worked successfully earlier. However, when redeploying these services as duplicate apps for testing, BAS incorrectly interprets them as V2 services. As a result, the generated application is broken and fails to function both during preview in BAS and after deployment.

The app fails to load with the following errors:
"[ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed"

"Invalid metadata document."

It is important to note that the same application and service work perfectly fine when previewed locally through Eclipse, which confirms that both the app and the generated OData V4 service, metadata are correct. 

Is anyone else facing a similar problem? App generation and deployment has worked absolutely fine in the past with the exact same steps.

Best Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entities but only shows Custom Entities
entry.content:
Hi guys,

When creating a CDS View with the purpose of using it as a Code List in a Custom Field I found the following:

If I create the CDS view as a View Entity, it's not shown in the "Code List based on CDS View".

This is the code I'm using to create the CDS View (I Released it for Use in Key User Apps (C1 Contract) )

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Freight Codes'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP
@Search.searchable: true
@ObjectModel.representativeKey: 'FreightCode'

define view entity Y43MM_VH_FreightCode
  as select from y43mm_frgtcode
{
      @search.defaultSearchElement: true
      @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
      @search.ranking: #LOW
  key freight_code      as FreightCode,
  
      @search.defaultSearchElement: true
      @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
      @search.ranking: #LOW  
      freight_code_desc as FreightCodeDesc
}

But if I change the CDS View to be a Custom Entity, the view is correctly shown in the "Code List based on CDS View"

@EndUserText.label: 'Freight Codes'
@ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP
@Search.searchable: true
@ObjectModel.representativeKey: 'FreightCode'
@ObjectModel: {
    query: {
        implementedBy: 'ABAP:Y43TMCL_CE_FREIGHTCODE'
    }
}
define custom entity Y43TM_VH_FREIGHTCODE
{
      @search.defaultSearchElement: true
      @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
      @search.ranking: #LOW
  key FreightCode: y43tmdt_freight_code;
  
      @search.defaultSearchElement: true
      @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
      @search.ranking: #LOW  
      FreightCodeDesc: y43tmdt_freight_code_desc;
}

As additional information:

  • This behavior only occurs in S/4HANA Cloud Public edition. If I create a View entity in an On-Premise system 2023, the CDS view is correctly shown in the "Code List based on CDS View"
  • I read the information on the following link Creating a Field of the Type Code List Based on CDS View: Example in point 4 it perfectly says "You can either select a CDS-based view, or you can select a custom entity". So, as per my understanding, it should work ok with View Entities in Cloud as well. So I'm not sure what's going on.

Let me know if you have any information that could help me with this problem.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Hybrid Local Development with SAP Cloud Foundry: Access On-Premises APIs via the Cloud Connector
entry.content:
When building full-stack applications on SAP BTP, debugging against on-premise backends is a common requirement. This post outlines a simple method to simulate the connectivity available in the BTP Cloud Foundry runtime on your local machine, enabling access to on-premise APIs through the BTP Subaccount and Cloud Connector.

Scenario:

Imagine you're developing an AI Agent and need to create tools that interact with an on-premise API. In line with SAP best practices, this API is exposed to your Subaccount via the Cloud Connector. While deployed applications can access the API directly, how do you test this setup locally?

The solution is to use the Cloud Foundry CLI tooling to establish a tunnel to the Cloud Foundry environment, routing traffic through it. This allows local development and debugging as though you're working directly within the BTP runtime.

Concept: SAP Cloud Connector:

The SAP Cloud Connector (SCC) acts as a secure bridge between on-premise systems and cloud applications. It allows cloud applications, such as those running on SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform), to access resources in on-premise environments securely. The connector ensures data transfer via encrypted communication, maintaining the integrity and confidentiality of sensitive information. It supports fine-grained access control, enabling administrators to define which systems, services, or data can be exposed to the cloud. Additionally, SCC simplifies integration by avoiding the need for complex network configurations like VPNs. This makes it a critical tool for hybrid cloud setups, enabling seamless and secure connectivity.

Example Setup:

For this example, I configured a Cloud Connector on a machine and exposed an HTTP-based sample server. You can refer to this detailed blog post here to see the process outlined in Step 1.

The setup results in the virtualhost:3333 being securely exposed to my BTP Subaccount.

Taking the script from that blog as an example, I make an HTTP request to the exposed virtualhost:3333:

 

def example_http_request(host, port, path, auth_token, location_id, proxy_host):
    """
    Performs an HTTP GET request through SAP Cloud Connector.
    """
    url = f"http://{host}:{port}{path}"
    headers = {
        "Proxy-Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}",
        "SAP-Connectivity-SCC-Location_ID": location_id
    }
    proxies = {"http": proxy_host}    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, proxies=proxies, verify=False)
    return response

 

 

Doing the request on BTP, we receive the expected "Hello World" response. Running the same request on my local machine, however, would result in a connection error, as the on-premise API is not directly accessible from the local environment.

 

An error occurred: HTTPConnectionPool(host='connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com', port=20003): Max retries exceeded with url: http://virtualhost:3333/hello (Caused by ProxyError('Unable to connect to proxy', ConnectTimeoutError(<urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x00000191F0BFF6B0>, 'Connection to connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com timed out. (connect timeout=None)')))

 

Note: No surprise there, as the connectivity proxy is an internal host: connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com.

 

Setting Up a Tunnel to a Sample Application

There’s a light at the end of the tunnel: the Cloud Foundry Command Line Interface offers the ability to create an SSH tunnel for deployed apps, as described in the documentation here. This feature can be useful in various scenarios. One example is accessing a database deployed within the environment, which is not exposed to the outside world—only your internal apps can access its ports. Another example is our case: developing locally against an on-premise backend.

However, this can only be done in relation to an app. You can either use your own existing app or a sample dummy app. In this case, I’ll use a sample app, which I’ve named myapp, based on the code available here.

After quickly running cf push, the deployment is successfully completed.

 

Waiting for app myapp to start...Instances starting...
Instances starting...
Instances starting...
Instances starting...name:              myapp
requested state:   started
routes:            myapp-brash-warthog-cj.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com
last uploaded:     Wed 18 Dec 21:43:35 CET 2024
stack:             cflinuxfs4
buildpacks:
        name               version   detect output   buildpack name
        python_buildpack   1.8.30    python          pythontype:            web
sidecars:
instances:       1/1
memory usage:    128M
start command:   python hello.py
     state     since                  cpu    memory   disk     details
#0   running   2024-12-18T20:43:49Z   0.0%   0 of 0   0 of 0

 

Having deployed the app we can then establish the SSH tunnel using the following command:

 

cf ssh -L 8888:connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com:20003 myapp

 

Lets break this down:

  • cf: Refers to the Cloud Foundry CLI.
  • ssh: Invokes the SSH feature of the Cloud Foundry CLI.
  • -L 8888:connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com:20003:
    This specifies the SSH tunnel with the following parts:
    • 8888: The local port on your machine where the tunnel will be accessible.
    • connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com: The target host inside the Cloud Foundry environment.
    • 20003: The target port on the remote host within the Cloud Foundry environment.
  • myapp: The name of the application you want to connect to via SSH.

Essentially that leaves us with a tunnel that is reachable from localhost:8888 - forwarding our traffic to the internal connectivity proxy host at its http proxy port 20003.

Running the ssh tunnel command now gives us access to the runtime environment of the app:

 

PS C:\Github\sap-cloud-foundry-python-cloud-connector> cf ssh -L 8888:connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com:20003 myapp
vcap@3e707408-4eca-41f4-7dee-d848:~$ ls
app  deps  logs  profile.d  staging_info.yml  tmp
vcap@3e707408-4eca-41f4-7dee-d848:~$
vcap@3e707408-4eca-41f4-7dee-d848:~$

 

Running the Local Script via the Tunnel

While the SSH session is active, the tunnel remains open. However, there’s a small adjustment we need to make: instead of using connectivityproxy.internal... as our proxy host URL, we must reference localhost:8888. This requires updating the corresponding value in the secret. Once that’s done, we can run the script seamlessly—whether through a debugger or by executing it with the Python interpreter in a separate command-line process. It should now work as expected.

 

PS C:\Github\sap-cloud-foundry-python-cloud-connector> python app.py
Fetching OAuth token...
Token acquired successfully.
Sending HTTP request through SAP Cloud Connector...
Response received:
{"message":"Hello World 2024-12-18T20:46:49.181Z"}
Script execution completed.

 

Great! We've seen how to connect to on-premise systems from our local environment using the cf ssh tunneling options to route traffic through the Cloud Connector.

This approach works for both HTTP and TCP traffic. Feel free to check out my other blog on how to proxy TCP traffic using Python.

entry.title: SAP Signavio for FS-CD (Financial Services Collections & Disbursements)
entry.content:
About SAP Signavio

Business process transformation from SAP Signavio enables companies to understand, improve, and transform their business processes from an end-to-end perspective. Because the solutions are web-based, they enable rapid, scalable transformation of all business processes, across all areas of the enterprise.

Accordingly, SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite includes the following core capabilities: 

  • Process analysis and process mining:End-to-end process analysis for business transformations and initiatives regarding operational excellence.
  • Process and journey modeling:Management, modeling, and simulation of business processes and diverse journeys.
  • Process governance and automation:Ensuring organizational and regulatory compliance of all documented processes.
  • Process collaboration:Interactive and cross-divisional collaboration in real time.

Source: SAP-Press.com

 

SAP Signavio Process Navigator

Best practice processes can be accessed via the SAP Signavio Process Navigator. Since FS-CD is an industry component of Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable, the processes can be found, for example, by searching for Contract Accounting.

The following scope items are available and could be adopted for FS-CD:

Contract Accounting - Cash Application (47I)

In this process, when you transfer your bank statement to payment lots, standard rule-based proposals are generated and the automatic posting and clearing is executed. If you've activated this scope item, the new machine learning service is triggered for payment lot items where the clearing isn't successfully completed. This service proposes selection categories and selection values for triggering the FI-CA clearing control. If you choose to trigger the automatic clearing and the confidence level is above the threshold, the FI-CA clearing control is automatically processed with consideration to the machine learning proposals.

Contract Accounting - Group Ledger IFRS (33X)

In this scope item, you handle ledger-specific transactions and reports for IFRS within contract accounting. This includes Doubtful Receivables, Foreign Currency Valuation, Day-End Closing, and Month-End Closing.

Contract Accounting: Open Item Management (PUJ)

In this scope item, you handle open item management for contract accounting.
Source: SAP Signavio Process Navigator

 

 
This includes dunning, installment plans, interest calculation, and credit clarification.

Contract Accounting - Daily and Monthly Closing (2BK)

Daily and monthly closing let you prepare and carry out the activities required for day-end and month-end closing for Convergent Contract Accounting (FI-CAC). The major activity for closing is the reconciliation between Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable and General Ledger (FI-GL). In addition, you can write off uncollectible receivables in Convergent Contract Accounting (FI-CAC).

Contract Accounting - Company Initiated Payments (2DP)

This scope item allows you to start the collect payment procedure from company perspective. Using different methods, the company can receive the customer’s payment and perform further processing.

Contract Accounting - Customer Initiated Payments (2BI)

Paid invoice processing allows you to post invoices that are already paid. You can also manage the payment card settlement process, ending in either a successful transfer to the General Ledger (FI-GL) or returns processing. The payment card process requires an integration of Convergent Contract Accounting (FI-CAC) and Payment Hub (digital payments).

Contract Accounting - Master Data and Basic Functions (2AR)

Business Partner and Contract Management lets you create and manage master data of a business partner and a contract account for Convergent Contract Accounting (FI-CAC). In addition, you can analyze if business partner data can be deleted to satisfy data privacy constraints in SAP S/4HANA. Furthermore, you can create and print correspondence.

SAP Fiori Analytical Apps for Contract Accounting Manager (2KH)

This scope item lets you analyze data in Convergent Contract Accounting (FI-CAC) according to different business perspectives.

Contract Accounting - External Tax Audit and Compliance Reporting (2SJ)

This scope item allows internal and external auditors to view, analyze, and extract accounting relevant data from Convergent Contract Accounting (FI-CAC). Accounting data covers the relevant customizing extracts, business partner extracts, and FI-CAC documents. Extracts are exported by the Administrator and are analyzed by the External Auditor. Extracts can also be imported in standard tax auditor software. The three legally required access types (direct system access, indirect system access, and file access) are supported.

 

SAP Signavio Value Accelerator

In addition to the scope items, value accelerators are also available. The recent release by BearingPoint of a new value accelerator for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence that supports a core and high impact process for insurers: the optimization of payments for Premium Collections in SAP Collections and Disbursements for Insurance.

The Accelerator brings the essential business processes from FS-CD into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

  • Incoming Payments monitoring:
    • Control: DSO, outstanding amounts,
    • Clarification case analysis
  • Dunning: analyses + dashboards:
    • Allocation of dunning fees/charges
    • Classification of customers
    • Optimization of dunning levels
    • Tracking and monitoring
    • Reporting, analysis
    • Customer communication
    • Submission to debt collection / Collection agency 
  • Returns:
    • Categorization of reasons for returns
    • Bank communication
    • Follow-up processes and multiple returns
  • Broker collection:
    • Broker report
    • Broker collection transaction data: premium, commission, claims
    • Credit clarification cases on individual or commission contracts

 

Although the scope items were created for FI-CA, they still offer added value, especially for implementation projects or S/4HANA transformations. BearingPoint's accelerator, on the other hand, supports insurance processes such as broker collection and delivers initial analyses within a few days. Business processes can be optimized on the basis of these.

What do you think about this or have you already had initial experiences with SAP Signavio for FS-CD? If so, please write to me in the comments.

 

Additional links:

  • SAP Signavio Process Navigator
  • Value accelerator library for SAP Signavio
  • SAP Fioneer FS-CD 
  • BearingPoint releases SAP Signavio Value Accelerator that optimizes payments for Premium Collections

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content:
Update

Diagnosis

Custom fields of type "Code List based on CDS View" must not reference custom CDS views, which use artefacts defined via ADT development. The key user extensibility concept guarantees lifecycle-stable extensions, which are upgrade-proof. But ADT artefacts are managed by customers and might contain errors which prevent an activation. Therefore, custom fields need to be decoupled from ADT development artefacts to ensure successful lifecycle-events.

System Response

The creation of the custom field is being prevented as long as the select CDS view itself or a view in a lower layer of the view stack, uses artefacts defined via ADT development.



I have same issue. Custom entity comes to list but cds views are not.

I copied one of the listed CDS views (for example C_BOMMaterialVH to ZC_BOMMaterialVH) and gives C1 contract but still not listed.

For second option, I try to create cds view which includes my Z view by using "Custom CDS View" app but it is still not listed.

Because there is a filter "Recommended Data Source". When remove that filter, my custom CDS view is listed. Then new cds view which starts with YY1 is listed custom field option

entry.title: Purchase Order Carry Forward Budget Carry Forward
entry.content:
Currently, when we carry forward the purchase orders at the end of the fiscal year, the corresponding budget amount is carried forward as well. It works well for the departments that expect the purchase order will be invoiced by the end of the year, but causes issues for purchase orders that are multi year and budgeted for each fiscal year.

Is there a way for the system to identify which purchase orders should have the budget carried forward and which ones should not due to it being for multiple fiscal years?   

Thank you.
